在宅勤務や外出先からの業務でファイル共有を安全に進めるために、送信前・共有中・完: A practical checklist for safe file sharing while working from home or on the go, covering what to check before sending, during sharing, and after completion.
送るファイルは必要最小限か: Are you sending only the files that are truly necessary?
ファイルを送った後は、相手が受け取れたか、作業に必要な状態で開けたかを確認します: After sending, confirm that the recipient received the file and could open it in a usable state. In remote work, you can't see the other person's screen or connection status, so a failed download is easy to miss. For important documents, always ask for a quick "Got it" confirmation, and if needed, verify the file name, page count, or hash to rule out any mix-up.
リモートワークでのファイル共有は、オフィス内の受け渡しよりも確認すべき点が増えま: Sharing files in remote work involves more potential pitfalls than handing something over in the office. Recipients are on different networks, with different devices and connection quality. Notifications get buried in chat and email. Once a file is shared, it's hard to know where it ends up. When all these factors combine, even sending a single document can lead to small but costly mishaps — misdirected files, expired links, outdated versions being referenced, or sharing links left dangling indefinitely.
最初に確認したいのは、送ろうとしているファイルの中身です。リモートワークでは「ひ: Start by reviewing what's actually inside the file you're about to send. In remote work, it's tempting to just send a whole folder or bundle related documents together — but the wider the sharing scope, the greater the potential damage if something leaks. Before sending, select only the files the recipient genuinely needs, and check that nothing extraneous has crept in: revision history, personal information, internal notes, draft versions, or old quote terms.
また、共有が終わったファイルは残さないことが基本です。自動削除が使える場合は、共: As a general rule, don't leave files around after sharing is done. If auto-delete is available, set the expiry date before you share. If you're deleting manually, agree in advance on who is responsible — does the sender delete it, or does the recipient report back once they've saved what they need? Defining a clear end condition for each share reduces the risk of links being forgotten and left open.
毎回長い手順書を読んでから共有するのは現実的ではありません。チームで使うなら、次: Reading through a lengthy procedure every time you share a file isn't realistic. If you're doing this as a team, keeping a short checklist as a pinned message or template in your chat is much more practical. Have the sender run through it before each share, and pass it along to the recipient when needed — it keeps everyone's judgment consistent, even in remote settings.
特に表計算ファイルやプレゼン資料は、非表示シート、コメント、スピーカーノート、変: Spreadsheets and presentation files in particular can harbor data in hidden sheets, comments, speaker notes, and revision histories. Decide upfront whether the document should be converted to PDF before sending, or whether the recipient genuinely needs an editable source file. If they only need to view it, don't hand over an editable version.
重要なのは、共有リンクだけを投げるのではなく、相手が何をすればよいかを一文添える: Don't just drop a link — add a sentence explaining what you need the recipient to do. For example: "Here's today's review PDF. You only need to reference it during the meeting, so no need to save it afterwards." Or: "This is the latest estimate. Please download this version so there's no mix-up with older files." Pairing purpose with handling instructions aligns everyone's expectations.
リモートワークでは、共有リンクがチャット、メール、議事録、タスク管理ツールなど複: In remote work, sharing links have a tendency to accumulate — in chat threads, emails, meeting notes, and task boards. Convenient as that is, links that outlive their purpose become a liability. The basic principle of file sharing is to keep the audience as narrow as possible and the availability window as short as the job requires.
共有時：相手が迷わない受け取り方を用意する: During Sharing: Make It Easy for the Recipient to Know What to Do
ファイル名だけで内容と版が分かる状態になっているか: Can the content and version be identified from the file name alone?
受け取り確認と共有終了のタイミングを決めているか: Have you agreed on when to confirm receipt and when to end the share?
保存期間は作業に必要な長さだけにしているか: Is the retention period set to only as long as the work requires?
ファイル名に日付、用途、版が入っているか: Does the file name include the date, purpose, and version?
リモートワークでは「送れること」より「安全に終われること」が重要: In Remote Work, "Finishing Safely" Matters More Than "Being Able to Send"
リモートワークのファイル共有は、特別な知識よりも小さな確認の積み重ねが効きます。: Safe file sharing in remote work comes down to small, consistent checks — not specialized knowledge. Use a temporary sharing tool like FS!QR and build the habit of running through your pre-send, mid-share, and post-completion checks, and you can raise your security standard without sacrificing speed.
機密性が高い資料は、パスワード保護やID確認を組み合わせると安心です。共有URL: For highly sensitive documents, combining password protection with ID verification adds an extra layer of reassurance. Sending the URL in chat and the password via a separate channel — so everything isn't travelling the same route — is also effective. For quick handoffs, there's no reason to set a long retention period. Choose a sharing method on the principle of not leaving anything around once the transfer is complete.
共有相手が作業に必要なファイルだけを選んでいるか: Have you selected only the files the recipient needs for their work?
一時共有の考え方を使うと、旧版リンクを長く残さずに済みます。短い期間だけファイル: Adopting a temporary-sharing mindset means old version links don't linger. Keep files up for just long enough, close the share once the intended recipient has what they need, and you significantly reduce the chance of outdated links being accessed later. The key distinction: treat file sharing as a passage for handoffs, not as a storage location.
日常運用に落とし込むためのチェックリスト: A Checklist You Can Actually Use Every Day
完了後：共有した事実と削除を確認する: After Completion: Confirm the Share Happened and the File Is Gone
FS!QRのようにブラウザとQRコードで一時的に共有できる仕組みは、相手にアプリ: A browser- and QR-code-based temporary sharing system like FS!QR is a natural fit for remote environments because it doesn't require the other party to install any app. That said, no matter what tool you use, vague practices before and after sharing will compromise your security. This article lays out the checkpoints to help you share files quickly and reliably whether you're working from home or on the go.
機密情報が含まれる場合、パスワードや別経路連絡を使っているか: If the file contains confidential information, are you using a password or a separate channel?
リモート環境では、共有リンクを送っただけでは作業が止まることがあります。受け手が: In remote settings, just sending a sharing link can sometimes bring work to a halt. The best way for someone to receive a file depends on whether they're checking on a phone, downloading on a PC, using a company device, or a personal one. With FS!QR you can share via URL or display the QR code on screen, making it easy to say "take a look at this document now" during an online meeting.
保護設定：公開範囲と利用時間を短くする: Protection Settings: Narrow the Audience and Shorten the Window
非表示データ、コメント、不要な個人情報が残っていないか: Are there any hidden data, comments, or unnecessary personal information still in the file?
リモートチームでは、同じ資料の修正版が何度も共有されることがあります。ファイル名: Remote teams often share multiple revised versions of the same document. When file names start piling up with "Final," "Latest," "Rev2," and so on, it becomes impossible to know which one to open — and decisions get made based on stale content. Always include the date and purpose in the file name before sharing, and clearly state "This is the latest version" in the chat message.

共有中：最新版と旧版を混在させない: During Sharing: Never Mix Current and Previous Versions
